finding_results.notes: Replaced the two-negations-plus-incredulous-question ladder ('You were not performing the ritual correctly. You were not performing it at all. You were — what were you doing?') with a scene-specific field assessment that names what Aldric actually heard from the residue side: the gibberish incantation (numbers and unrecognizable fragments), the genuine supplication form held over a concentrated contamination field with nothing sealed into it, and the impossible fact that the opening seated anyway. The new line keeps Aldric formal and morally certain (no contractions, complete sentences, classificatory language) while grounding his objection in concrete details visible in this chapter — the fake murmuring described at line 19, the postures copied from the woodcut at line 17, and the real opening at line 31. The calibration example pattern is fully eliminated.

summary: Single finding addressed. Aldric's ditch accusation at line 81 was rewritten from a generalized negation ladder echoing the style bible calibration line into a concrete, scene-specific complaint naming the gibberish incantation, the empty supplication posture, and the impossible opening — all details drawn from the ritual sequence already established in the chapter. No other lines were changed; the surrounding exchange (lines 77-93) retains its existing texture and the rest of the chapter is unmodified.
